what_it_does: A working-grade parent-theory note that surfaces a single 'parametric substrate operation' it claims underlies the project corpus. It proposes an eight-parameter tuple (R, U, θ, m, n, x, k, Rule), maps it onto parent theories, type signatures, monodromy, eigenpoints, the structural/operational/semantic faces, bootstrap self-application, a seven-piece synthesis of existing mathematical frameworks, and cross-domain rule-configurations, while marking many open seams.
observations: Declares status 'working-grade' and 'candidacy throughout' with explicit open seams.
Defines the operation as Op(P) = Rule(P; R, U, θ, m, n, x, k).
Decomposes four named parent theories across parameter clusters and predicts two unnamed ones: 'coherent subdivision' and 'triangulated selection'.
Proposes a universal type signature Type(t) = (R, U, θ, m, n, x; k; Rule_t).
Connects winding number k to monodromy, the six-state lifecycle, and distinct absence.
Defines eigenpoints as positions where monodromy acts trivially and introduces an 'eigenpoint lattice'.
Maps structural/operational/semantic faces to which parameter coordinates are fixed vs variable.
Frames the bootstrap process as the same operation applied recursively at conversation, artefact, corpus, source-estate, and engine scales.
Sketches a seven-piece synthesis of existing mathematics: indexed categories, Poly, operads, simplicial sets with monodromy, Markov categories, coeffects, modal type theory.
Includes a cross-domain table showing domains as rule-configurations of the same operation.
Attributes model authorship to Tim Geldard and AI contribution to 'articulation-amplification'.
standouts: The eight-parameter tuple presented as a universal substrate operation.
Parent-theory decomposition plus two predicted missing parent theories.
conventions: Uses Obsidian/wiki-link references to other project files.
Heavy use of 'candidacy register', 'open seams', and 'working-grade' status markers.
Section numbering with anchor IDs like ^p1-s1.
Repeated 'the integration surfaces' framing across sections.
concerns: Most claims are explicitly held as candidacy or open seams, making the file hard to falsify.
Very dense jargon and heavy reliance on cross-referenced files; not self-contained.
Formal sketches such as the 'Substrate-mathematics' composition formula are admitted as unverified working sketches.
Some sections are skeletal or proto-state.
notes: A dense, candidacy-register working note proposing an eight-parameter substrate operation that unifies the project's parent theories, type signatures, monodromy, eigenpoints, and cross-domain rule-configurations.
questions: What is the precise formal definition of Rule as a morphism or function?
How would the eight parameters be measured or computed in a concrete implementation?
What distinguishes the two predicted parent theories from existing notes that might already cover them?
How does the monodromy group act on the full parameter tuple beyond orientation inheritance?
What criteria would move this articulation from candidacy to settled?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to surface the parametric substrate operation the corpus has been articulating; the file does that, integrating across many notes and marking open seams, so it matches its stated purpose.
apparent_themes: Parametric universality: one operation with rule-varying instantiations.
Monodromy and winding number as modal/lifecycle structure.
Assembly of existing mathematical apparatus rather than invention of new theory.
Candidacy register and explicit open seams as epistemic discipline.
intention_signals: It attempts to unify the entire project corpus under a single formal operation, showing that disparate notes are projections of one parametric substrate operation, and to outline a research programme to formalise that operation using existing mathematical frameworks.
novelty: Distinctive in proposing an explicit eight-parameter universal operation tuple that subsumes parent theories, type signatures, monodromy, and cross-domain instantiations in one schema, plus predicting two missing parent theories.
contribution: This file contributes a high-level integrative schema (the parametric operation and its parameter tuple) that links parent theories, type signatures, monodromy, eigenpoints, three-face decomposition, bootstrap self-application, and a seven-piece mathematical synthesis; it also explicitly catalogues open seams and candidacy status, making its own limits visible.
summary_for_embedding: A working-grade parent-theory note proposing an eight-parameter 'parametric substrate operation' that unifies the project's corpus across parent-theory decomposition, universal type signatures, monodromy/eigenpoint structure, three-face variability, bootstrap self-application, seven-piece synthesis mathematics, and cross-domain rule-configurations.
intention_for_embedding: To surface and formalise the single parametric operation that the author believes underlies all prior project articulations, and to map its parameters onto existing mathematical apparatus while marking open research seams.
